name: Volodymyr Zelenskyy
bio: Former comedian and actor turned politician, elected President of Ukraine in 2019. Has led the country through Russia's full-scale invasion since February 2022, becoming a prominent figure in international diplomacy and wartime leadership.

quotes.text: It is not serious to expect Ukraine to observe a ceasefire timed to a Russian military holiday while receiving no official notice from Moscow.
They want permission from Ukraine to hold their parade, so they can safely march onto the square for one hour once a year, and then go back to killing our people and waging war again.

Such a Russia can never be normalized. Ukraine will not allow any of the aggressor's strikes that take the lives of our people to go unpunished. We are entirely justified in our responses against Russia's oil industry, weapons industry and those directly responsible for committing war crimes against Ukraine and Ukrainians.
quotes.source: NBC News / Kyiv Post — remarks at Kyiv apartment building strike site
quotes.topic: Retaliatory strike plan after Kyiv apartment-block strike kills 24

quotes.text: Russia must feel the cost of every strike on Ukrainian civilians. Ryazan is one answer. There will be others. We will keep working with President Trump on a written peace; we're now at 90 to 95 per cent agreement on the proposal. The remaining gap is Moscow.
quotes.source: Office of the President of Ukraine — paraphrased remarks reported by Reuters/Bloomberg
quotes.topic: Ryazan refinery strike and US-mediated written peace proposal status

quotes.text: The strikes are entirely justified. Russia took 24 lives in a Kyiv apartment block, including three children. Ryazan was the economic answer on Saturday. Moscow's airspace overnight is the political one. We remain committed to the written peace at 90 to 95 per cent — the gap is Moscow, not us.
quotes.source: Office of the President of Ukraine — Sunday May 17 readout, via CBC News, Fortune and Reuters wire
quotes.topic: 500+ drone strike on Moscow region — largest single-night Ukrainian operation since 2022 invasion
